fields.name: __account__
fields.label: AWS account
fields.help: Active AWS account (set by the active simulator space).

fields.name: instance_type
fields.label: Instance type
fields.help: Instance type from the simulator's AWS catalog — host-clamped on launch.

fields.name: key_name
fields.label: Key pair name
fields.help: Leave blank to proceed without a key pair (simulator generates SSH access via lxc exec).

fields.name: __keyAck__
fields.label: Proceed without a key pair
fields.help: Required if Key pair name is blank — confirms you understand SSH/RDP login won't be available.

fields.name: __vpc__
fields.label: VPC
fields.help: VPC ID; default VPC is auto-created in the simulator.

fields.name: __allowHttp__
fields.label: Allow HTTPS traffic from the internet

fields.name: __allowHttpsBasic__
fields.label: Allow HTTP traffic from the internet

fields.name: iam_instance_profile
fields.label: IAM instance profile
fields.help: ARN or name of an IAM role to attach to this instance.

fields.name: __detailedMon__
fields.label: Enable detailed monitoring
fields.help: Sends metric data every 1 minute instead of 5 minutes (extra cost in real AWS).

fields.name: user_data
fields.label: User data (cloud-init script)
fields.help: Base-64 encoded shell script that runs on first boot.

fields.name: __help__
fields.value: Tags are key-value pairs that help organize, search, and apply policies to your AWS resources.

fields.name: __account__
fields.label: AWS account
fields.help: Active AWS account (set by the active simulator space).

validate.message: 3-63 chars, lowercase, digits, hyphens, periods; globally unique

fields.name: __copySettings__
fields.label: Copy settings from existing bucket
fields.help: Optional — bucket name to clone settings from.

sections.label: Block Public Access settings for this bucket

fields.name: __blockAll__
fields.label: Block all public access
fields.help: Strongly recommended — turning off requires explicit acknowledgement.

fields.name: __ack__
fields.label: I acknowledge that the current settings might result in this bucket becoming public.

options.label: Server-side encryption with Amazon S3 managed keys (SSE-S3)
Server-side encryption with AWS Key Management Service keys (SSE-KMS)
Dual-layer server-side encryption with AWS KMS keys (DSSE-KMS)

fields.name: __help__
fields.value: Tags are key-value pairs that help organize, search, and apply policies to your AWS resources.

fields.name: __account__
fields.label: AWS account
fields.help: Active AWS account (set by the active simulator space).

fields.name: __consoleAccess__
fields.label: Provide user access to the AWS Management Console
fields.help: If enabled, this user can sign in to the console with a password.

fields.name: __pwdReset__
fields.label: Users must create a new password at next sign-in

fields.name: permissions_boundary
fields.label: Permissions boundary
fields.help: Optional — ARN of a managed policy to set the maximum permissions for this user.

fields.name: __help__
fields.value: Tags are key-value pairs that help organize, search, and apply policies to your AWS resources.

fields.name: __account__
fields.label: AWS account
fields.help: Active AWS account (set by the active simulator space).

options.label: Production — defaults for HA + fast performance
Free tier — db.t3.micro, single AZ, no backups

validate.message: 1-63 chars, lowercase, must begin with a letter, hyphens but not consecutive
1-16 chars, letters/digits, start with a letter

options.label: Yes — RDS will assign a public IP address
No — RDS will not assign a public IP address

fields.name: __help__
fields.value: Tags are key-value pairs that help organize, search, and apply policies to your AWS resources.

fields.name: __account__
fields.label: AWS account
fields.help: Active AWS account (set by the active simulator space).

validate.message: 3-255 chars, letters, digits, underscore, dot, hyphen

fields.name: sort_key
fields.label: Sort key (optional)
fields.help: Combined with partition key forms the primary key. Leave blank for partition-key-only.

fields.name: __help__
fields.value: Tags are key-value pairs that help organize, search, and apply policies to your AWS resources.

fields.name: __account__
fields.label: AWS account
fields.help: Active AWS account (set by the active simulator space).

options.label: FIFO — exactly-once processing, strict FIFO order

validate.message: 1-80 chars, letters/digits/hyphens/underscores; FIFO queues must end with .fifo

fields.name: __help__
fields.value: Tags are key-value pairs that help organize, search, and apply policies to your AWS resources.

fields.name: __account__
fields.label: AWS account
fields.help: Active AWS account (set by the active simulator space).

options.label: Create a new role with basic Lambda permissions
Create a new role from AWS policy templates

fields.name: code
fields.label: Function code
fields.default:
def lambda_handler(event, context):
    return {'statusCode': 200, 'body': 'Hello from Lambda!'}
fields.help: Inline Python code or paste any runtime's entry-point.

fields.name: __help__
fields.value: Tags are key-value pairs that help organize, search, and apply policies to your AWS resources.

fields.name: __account__
fields.label: AWS account
fields.help: Active AWS account (set by the active simulator space).

validate.message: 3-63 chars, lowercase letters/digits/hyphens, start with a letter

fields.name: __seedHello__
fields.label: Seed sample MOCK /hello route
fields.help: Creates a starter /hello resource with a MOCK integration responding with {"message":"Hello, world"}.

fields.name: __help__
fields.value: Tags are key-value pairs that help organize, search, and apply policies to your AWS resources.

fields.name: __account__
fields.label: AWS account
fields.help: Active AWS account (set by the active simulator space).

validate.message: 3-63 chars, lowercase letters/digits/hyphens, start with a letter

fields.name: __help__
fields.value: When 'VPC, subnets, etc.' is selected on the first tab, AWS will create subnets across the chosen AZs. In VPC-only mode subnets are added later from the Subnets sub-blade.

fields.name: __help__
fields.value: Tags are key-value pairs that help organize, search, and apply policies to your AWS resources.

fields.name: __account__
fields.label: AWS account
fields.help: Active AWS account (set by the active simulator space).

validate.message: 1-64 chars, letters, digits, dots, underscores, hyphens

fields.name: event_source
fields.label: AWS service
fields.help: Examples: aws.ec2, aws.s3, aws.lambda, aws.rds

fields.name: event_pattern
fields.label: Event pattern (JSON)
fields.help: Pattern that incoming events must match to trigger this rule.

fields.name: schedule_expression
fields.label: Schedule expression
fields.help: Examples: rate(5 minutes), cron(0 12 * * ? *)

fields.name: __help__
fields.value: Tags are key-value pairs that help organize, search, and apply policies to your AWS resources.

fields.name: __account__
fields.label: AWS account
fields.help: Active AWS account (set by the active simulator space).

options.label: Other type of secret (API keys, OAuth tokens, etc.)

fields.name: __resourcePolicy__
fields.label: Edit permissions
fields.help: Resource policy controls which AWS accounts and IAM users can access this secret.

fields.name: __replicaRegions__
fields.label: Replica Regions (comma-separated)
fields.help: e.g. us-west-2,eu-west-1

fields.name: rotation_lambda_arn
fields.label: Lambda rotation function
fields.help: ARN of the Lambda that rotates this secret.

fields.name: __help__
fields.value: Tags are key-value pairs that help organize, search, and apply policies to your AWS resources.

fields.name: __account__
fields.label: AWS account
fields.help: Active AWS account (set by the active simulator space).

options.label: Symmetric — single key for both encrypt and decrypt

fields.name: multi_region
fields.label: Multi-Region key
fields.help: Replicate this key to other Regions later.

fields.name: __allowAdminsDelete__
fields.label: Allow key administrators to delete this key

ec2/events.label: Events
ec2/events.description: Account events affecting your EC2 resources (instance status changes, scheduled maintenance, etc.).

ec2/tag-editor.label: Tag Editor
ec2/tag-editor.description: Find and edit tags across multiple AWS resources at once.

ec2/instance-types.label: Instance Types
ec2/instance-types.description: All available EC2 instance types in this Region. Use as reference when picking sizes during launch.

ec2/launch-templates.label: Launch Templates
ec2/launch-templates.description: Reusable templates with instance configuration, applied at launch.

ec2/spot-requests.label: Spot Requests
ec2/spot-requests.description: Spot instance requests in this Region.

ec2/ami-catalog.label: AMI Catalog
ec2/ami-catalog.description: Curated Amazon and AWS Marketplace AMIs available in this Region.

ec2/volumes.label: Volumes (EBS)
ec2/volumes.description: Block storage volumes available to EC2 instances.

ec2/snapshots.label: Snapshots (EBS)
ec2/snapshots.description: Point-in-time copies of EBS volumes, stored in S3.

ec2/elastic-ips.label: Elastic IPs
ec2/elastic-ips.description: Static public IPv4 addresses you can attach to instances and network interfaces.

ec2/key-pairs.label: Key Pairs
ec2/key-pairs.description: SSH/RDP key pairs you can use to connect to instances.

ec2/network-ifs.label: Network Interfaces
ec2/network-ifs.description: Virtual network interfaces attached to EC2 instances. One per primary network attachment.

ec2/load-balancers.label: Load Balancers
ec2/load-balancers.description: Application, Network, and Gateway load balancers in this Region.

ec2/target-groups.label: Target Groups
ec2/target-groups.description: Target groups define how a load balancer routes requests to registered targets.

ec2/asg.label: Auto Scaling Groups
ec2/asg.description: Auto Scaling groups automatically launch and terminate instances based on policies.

s3/directory.label: Directory buckets
s3/directory.description: S3 directory buckets store data using S3 Express One Zone storage class for high-throughput workloads.

s3/table-buckets.label: Table buckets
s3/table-buckets.description: S3 table buckets store analytics data in Apache Iceberg format for SQL querying.

s3/access-points.label: Access Points
s3/access-points.description: Network-aware access points simplify managing access to shared S3 datasets.

s3/mrap.label: Multi-Region Access Points
s3/mrap.description: Single global endpoint that routes requests to the bucket with lowest latency.

s3/batch-ops.label: Batch Operations
s3/batch-ops.description: Batch jobs that perform large-scale actions on lists of S3 objects.

s3/storage-lens.label: Storage Lens
s3/storage-lens.description: S3 Storage Lens provides organization-wide visibility into object storage usage and activity.

s3/block-public.label: Block Public Access (account)
s3/block-public.description: Account-level S3 Block Public Access settings. These apply on top of bucket and access-point settings.

fields.name: block_public_acls
fields.label: Block public access granted through new ACLs

fields.name: ignore_public_acls
fields.label: Block public access granted through any ACLs

fields.name: block_public_policy
fields.label: Block public access granted through new public bucket or access point policies

fields.name: restrict_public_buckets
fields.label: Block public and cross-account access to buckets and objects through any public bucket or access point policies

iam/dashboard.label: IAM Dashboard
iam/dashboard.description: Snapshot of IAM resources and security recommendations for your account.

iam/access-analyzer.label: Access Analyzer
iam/access-analyzer.description: IAM Access Analyzer identifies resources shared with external principals.

iam/credential-report.label: Credential report
iam/credential-report.description: Last credential report for IAM users in this account.

rds/dashboard.label: RDS Dashboard
rds/dashboard.description: Overview of your RDS databases and recent activity.

rds/performance.label: Performance Insights
rds/performance.description: Database load and top SQL queries across your DB instances.

rds/subnet-groups.label: Subnet groups
rds/subnet-groups.description: Collections of subnets that RDS uses to place DB instances in a VPC.

rds/param-groups.label: Parameter groups
rds/param-groups.description: Engine-specific configuration parameters applied to DB instances.

rds/option-groups.label: Option groups
rds/option-groups.description: Engine-specific feature options applied to DB instances.

rds/reserved.label: Reserved instances
rds/reserved.description: Capacity reservations that provide a discount over on-demand pricing.

rds/events.label: Events
rds/events.description: Recent RDS events for DB instances, snapshots, and parameter groups.

dynamodb/dashboard.label: DynamoDB Dashboard
dynamodb/dashboard.description: Account-level view of DynamoDB usage and activity.

dynamodb/indexes.label: Indexes
dynamodb/indexes.description: Global and Local Secondary Indexes across all your tables.

dynamodb/backups.label: Backups
dynamodb/backups.description: On-demand and continuous backups of your DynamoDB tables.

dynamodb/exports.label: Exports to S3
dynamodb/exports.description: Continuous and on-demand exports of table data to S3.

dynamodb/streams.label: Streams
dynamodb/streams.description: DynamoDB Streams capture item-level changes for replication or processing.

dynamodb/dax.label: DAX clusters
dynamodb/dax.description: DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) clusters provide microsecond read/write latency for cached items.

lambda/dashboard.label: Lambda Dashboard
lambda/dashboard.description: Account-wide Lambda function metrics and recent activity.

lambda/applications.label: Applications
lambda/applications.description: Applications group related Lambda functions and AWS resources.

lambda/layers.label: Layers
lambda/layers.description: Shared libraries and runtime dependencies you can attach to multiple Lambda functions.

lambda/code-signing.label: Code signing
lambda/code-signing.description: Code-signing configurations to verify function code authenticity.

apigateway/domains.label: Custom domain names
apigateway/domains.description: Custom domain names that you map to your APIs.

apigateway/vpc-links.label: VPC links
apigateway/vpc-links.description: VPC links connect API Gateway to private resources inside your VPC.

apigateway/client-certs.label: Client certificates
apigateway/client-certs.description: Client SSL certificates that API Gateway presents to backends.

apigateway/usage-plans.label: Usage plans
apigateway/usage-plans.description: Quota and throttle limits applied to API keys.

apigateway/api-keys.label: API keys
apigateway/api-keys.description: API keys for authenticated access to your APIs.

sqs/dlqs.label: Dead-letter queues
sqs/dlqs.description: Queues configured to receive messages that can't be processed by their source queues. Derived from queue redrive policies.

vpc/nat-gateways.label: NAT gateways
vpc/nat-gateways.description: NAT gateways enable instances in private subnets to reach the internet for outbound traffic.

vpc/egress-only-igw.label: Egress-only IGWs
vpc/egress-only-igw.description: IPv6-only egress gateways for outbound traffic from private subnets.

vpc/carrier-gw.label: Carrier gateways
vpc/carrier-gw.description: Carrier gateways route traffic between an AWS Wavelength Zone VPC and a telecom provider.

vpc/dhcp-options.label: DHCP option sets
vpc/dhcp-options.description: DHCP option sets allow you to customize options like DNS and NTP servers passed to instances.

vpc/elastic-ips.label: Elastic IPs
vpc/elastic-ips.description: Static public IPv4 addresses available for use in this VPC.

vpc/endpoints.label: Endpoints
vpc/endpoints.description: VPC endpoints allow private connectivity to AWS services without traversing the internet.

vpc/endpoint-services.label: Endpoint services
vpc/endpoint-services.description: Your own services that other AWS accounts can connect to via interface endpoints.

vpc/peering.label: Peering connections
vpc/peering.description: VPC peering connections between two VPCs in the same or different accounts/regions.

vpc/nacls.label: Network ACLs
vpc/nacls.description: Stateless subnet-level firewall rules. Default NACL allows all traffic.

vpc/reachability.label: Reachability Analyzer
vpc/reachability.description: Network analyses that verify whether traffic between two endpoints can be delivered.

eventbridge/rules.label: Rules
eventbridge/rules.description: Rules match incoming events and route them to one or more targets.

eventbridge/event-buses.label: Event buses
eventbridge/event-buses.description: Event buses receive events from AWS services, partners, and your own applications.

eventbridge/archives.label: Archives
eventbridge/archives.description: Capture events from an event bus for retention or replay.

eventbridge/replays.label: Replays
eventbridge/replays.description: Re-process archived events through your rules and targets.

eventbridge/connections.label: Connections
eventbridge/connections.description: OAuth or API key credentials for API destination targets.

eventbridge/api-destinations.label: API destinations
eventbridge/api-destinations.description: HTTPS endpoints to invoke when a rule matches.

eventbridge/endpoints.label: Global endpoints
eventbridge/endpoints.description: Failover-routing endpoints for cross-Region disaster recovery.

eventbridge/pipes.label: Pipes
eventbridge/pipes.description: Point-to-point integrations from a source (SQS, Kinesis) to a target (Lambda, Step Functions).

eventbridge/schema-registries.label: Schema registries
eventbridge/schema-registries.description: Discover, manage, and version schemas for events on your event buses.

secretsmanager/secrets.label: Secrets
secretsmanager/secrets.description: Encrypted secret values (DB credentials, API keys, OAuth tokens). KMS-encrypted at rest.

secretsmanager/rotation.label: Rotation
secretsmanager/rotation.description: Schedule and history of secret-rotation Lambda invocations.

secretsmanager/replicas.label: Replication
secretsmanager/replicas.description: Secrets replicated to additional AWS Regions for HA.

secretsmanager/rotation-functions.label: Rotation functions
secretsmanager/rotation-functions.description: Lambda functions that perform the rotation logic.

kms/keys.label: Customer managed keys
kms/keys.description: Keys you create and manage. Use for encrypt/decrypt/sign/verify and KMS-integrated AWS services.

kms/aws-managed-keys.label: AWS managed keys
kms/aws-managed-keys.description: KMS keys created and managed by AWS services on your behalf (cannot be deleted).

kms/aliases.label: Aliases
kms/aliases.description: Friendly names for KMS keys. Aliases can be re-pointed without changing apps.

kms/custom-key-stores.label: Custom key stores
kms/custom-key-stores.description: AWS CloudHSM-backed key stores for FIPS 140-2 Level 3 keys.

kms/audit-events.label: AWS Config events
kms/audit-events.description: Recent KMS key configuration changes tracked by AWS Config.
